history: Patient is a woman in her 60s
Known history of JAK2-positive essential thrombocythemia diagnosed 10 years earlier
No splenomegaly or hepatomegaly documented at initial presentation and preoperative evaluation
presentation: Presented with a one-week history of jaundice
Laboratory tests revealed elevated total bilirubin (1.21 mg/dL), direct bilirubin (0.4 mg/dL), indirect bilirubin (0.8 mg/dL), and alkaline phosphatase (119 U/L)
Peripheral blood values showed a white blood cell count of 10.15 × 103/μL, a red blood cell count of 12 × 106/μL, and a platelet count of 399 × 103/μL
diagnostics: Abdominal ultrasonography identified a single 1-cm gallstone
Resected gallbladder measured 7 × 7.3 cm with a wall thickness of 0.2 cm
Specimen contained an 8-mm gallstone and a 5-mm lymph node located in the gallbladder neck region
Histopathological examination of the gallbladder revealed features consistent with chronic cholecystitis
Histological evaluation of the lymph node demonstrated clusters of atypical cells with multinucleation and nuclear lobulation within fibrotic and hemorrhagic stroma
Dysplastic megakaryocytes were present focally, comprising approximately 10%–15% of the nodal tissue
No accompanying erythroid or myeloid hematopoietic precursors were identified
Immunohistochemical analysis demonstrated strong cytoplasmic positivity for CD61 (GPIIIa)
Atypical cells were negative for cytokeratin, CD34, CD45, CD3, CK7, CD20, CD68, S100, and CD15
outcome: Postoperative course was uneventful with no reported complications
Splenomegaly was reported during subsequent postoperative clinical follow-up
